# Atomic Learning: a plain-English edition of a real textbook

> We don't ask what kind of learner you are. We give every learner several grounded routes to the same idea. We also show you the receipts.

## Concepts in prerequisite order

- [Vectors](llms-full.txt#vectors): A vector is a fixed-length array whose elements are scalars.
- [Dot Product](llms-full.txt#dot-product): The dot product combines two vectors by summing products of elements at corresponding positions.
- [Matrix–Vector Products](llms-full.txt#matrix-vector-product): A matrix–vector product produces a vector whose elements are dot products between matrix rows and the input vector.
- [Softmax](llms-full.txt#softmax): Softmax transforms neural-network outputs into a valid probability distribution over discrete classes.
- [Queries, Keys, and Values](llms-full.txt#qkv): Attention derives weights from query–key compatibility and uses those weights to form a linear combination of values.
- [Attention Pooling over Variable-Sized Data](llms-full.txt#attention-pooling): Attention pooling can operate over databases of arbitrary size without changing the pooling procedure.
- [Self-Attention](llms-full.txt#self-attention): Self-attention lets every token attend to every other token in the same sequence.
- [Positional Encoding](llms-full.txt#positional-encoding): Positional information is supplied as an additional token-associated input because self-attention alone does not preserve sequence order.
- [Softmax Preserves Ordering](llms-full.txt#softmax-ordering): Softmax preserves the ordering of its inputs, so the largest input identifies the highest-probability class.
- [Vector Norms](llms-full.txt#vector-norm): A vector norm measures the magnitude or size of a vector.
